scope: Read-only P6 retrospective review of validation reports, machine gates, negative checks, and reviewer evidence.

findings.summary: Machine gates still risk accepting motion parity when Web frames are identical, because reduced-motion evidence can satisfy the current logic.
State and interaction parity checks allow broad aliases or mismatched mode context, which can turn existence of traces into PASS-like evidence.
Final packet evidence binds to head 1977cbc, while loop state and history terminal evidence stop earlier, creating traceability drift.

recommendations: Split evidence states into typed PASS, PARTIAL, BLOCKED, and HUMAN_REQUIRED instead of using generic PASS language.
Add negative and mutation tests for state, interaction, and motion gates before another full milestone.
Bind loop terminal state and final review packet to the same fixed head before external review.
